title: App Store Submission
description: Navigate the App Store Connect submission process, from app metadata and screenshots to review guidelines and release strategies.

sections.content:
## App Store Connect Setup

App Store Connect is the web portal where you manage every aspect of your app's presence on the App Store — from metadata and pricing to submissions and analytics.

### Creating Your App Record

Before uploading a build, create an app record in App Store Connect:

1. **App Name** — Must be unique and not already in use (30 character limit)
2. **Primary Language** — The default language for your app listing
3. **Bundle ID** — Must match the one in your Xcode project exactly
4. **SKU** — An internal identifier for your own tracking (not visible to users)

### App Metadata

Your App Store listing includes several critical fields:

- **Description** — Up to 4,000 characters explaining your app's value
- **Keywords** — Up to 100 characters, comma-separated, for App Store search
- **Screenshots** — Required for each supported device size
- **App Icon** — 1024x1024 px, no alpha channel, no rounded corners (iOS adds them)
- **Category** — Primary and secondary (e.g., Productivity, Business)
- **Age Rating** — Determined by a questionnaire about content
- **Privacy** — Privacy nutrition label declaring what data you collect

### Pricing and Availability

You choose a **price tier** (from Free to Tier 1 and beyond). Apple handles currency conversion and regional pricing. You can also set introductory offers, subscriptions, and custom availability per country.

sections.title: App Metadata Checklist
sections.starterCode:
struct AppStoreListing {
 let name: String
 let bundleID: String
 let version: String
 let description: String
 let keywords: [String]
 let priceTier: Int
}

let myApp = AppStoreListing(
 name: "SwiftMentor",
 bundleID: "com.example.SwiftMentor",
 version: "1.0.0",
 description: "Learn Swift interactively",
 keywords: ["swift", "coding", "tutorial", "programming"],
 priceTier: 0
)

print("App: \(myApp.name)")
print("Version: \(myApp.version)")
print("Bundle ID: \(myApp.bundleID)")
print("Price: \(myApp.priceTier == 0 ? "Free" : "Tier \(myApp.priceTier)")")
print("Keywords: \(myApp.keywords.joined(separator: ", "))")
sections.expectedOutput:
App: SwiftMentor
Version: 1.0.0
Bundle ID: com.example.SwiftMentor
Price: Free
Keywords: swift, coding, tutorial, programming
sections.solution:
struct AppStoreListing {
 let name: String
 let bundleID: String
 let version: String
 let description: String
 let keywords: [String]
 let priceTier: Int
}

let myApp = AppStoreListing(
 name: "SwiftMentor",
 bundleID: "com.example.SwiftMentor",
 version: "1.0.0",
 description: "Learn Swift interactively",
 keywords: ["swift", "coding", "tutorial", "programming"],
 priceTier: 0
)

print("App: \(myApp.name)")
print("Version: \(myApp.version)")
print("Bundle ID: \(myApp.bundleID)")
print("Price: \(myApp.priceTier == 0 ? "Free" : "Tier \(myApp.priceTier)")")
print("Keywords: \(myApp.keywords.joined(separator: ", "))")

sections.question: What is the maximum length for an App Store app description?
sections.explanation: App Store descriptions can contain up to 4,000 characters, giving you room to explain your app's features, use cases, and value proposition in detail.

sections.content:
## Submission & Review

Once your app record and build are ready in App Store Connect, you submit for review.

### The Submission Process

1. **Upload your build** via Xcode (Product > Archive > Distribute App) or `xcrun altool`
2. **Select the build** in App Store Connect after processing completes (usually 5–30 minutes)
3. **Complete all required metadata** — missing fields prevent submission
4. **Submit for review** — Apple's team evaluates your app

### App Store Review Guidelines

Apple enforces the **App Store Review Guidelines** covering safety, performance, business, design, and legal requirements. Common rejection reasons include:

- **Crashes and bugs** — App must be functional and stable
- **Broken links** — All links (support, privacy policy) must work
- **Placeholder content** — No "Lorem ipsum" or unfinished features
- **Missing privacy policy** — Required if your app collects any data
- **In-app purchase misuse** — Digital goods must use Apple's IAP system
- **Misleading metadata** — Screenshots and description must match the app

### Review Timeline

- **Standard review**: 1–3 days for most apps
- **Expedited review**: Available for urgent fixes (limited use)
- **Rejection resolution**: Fix the issue, resubmit, and review typically takes less time

### Release Options

When your app is approved, you choose how to release:

- **Manual Release** — You control the exact release date
- **Automatic Release** — App goes live as soon as approved
- **Phased Release** — Roll out over 7 days to increasing percentages (1%, 2%, 5%, 10%, 20%, 50%, 100%) to catch issues early

sections.question: What does phased release do?
options: Releases the app only to internal testers
Gradually rolls out the update to increasing percentages of users over 7 days
Submits the app for expedited review
Releases the app only in select countries
sections.explanation: Phased release gradually rolls out your app update to increasing percentages of users over 7 days (1%, 2%, 5%, 10%, 20%, 50%, 100%), letting you monitor for crashes and issues before reaching your full audience.
